Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-11 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker Dashboard Today's data shows an extreme outlier in ERAS, which ranks #1 with a composite score of 97.6—driven primarily by extraordinary long-term returns (978% over one year and 851% over six months) rather than sustainable short-term consistency. However, red flags emerge in the details: ERAS shows a "thin" volatility profile (vol_ratio 0.511), weak correlations across most sectors (predominantly negative, bottoming at -0.171 with utilities), and a concerning momentum_signal of -0.47 despite the "clean" flag, suggesting recent momentum may be deteriorating despite historical gains. In sharp contrast, CNQ ranks #2 with more grounded fundamentals—steady 75.6% annual returns, all SMA gates passing, superior resilience on bad SPY days (0.51 vs ERAS's 1.54), and a sector-aligned correlation profile (0.615 with XLE energy sector), indicating more stable and defensible momentum. The dashboard reveals a critical tension: ERAS represents speculative bubble-like momentum with fragile foundations, while CNQ demonstrates classic momentum characteristics—consistent ratios, broad market outperformance, and structural support from sector trends.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
